issues.claim: the buddhi that was previously perplexed by the divergent teachings of the Veda — Shankara's reading
issues.objection: The engineering body explicitly cites Shankara's reading and the source pack records the striking commentarial divergence: Shankara reads śruti as 'Veda' (the buddhi tossed about by divergent Vedic teachings); Ramanuja makes a different lexical move and reads śruti as 'hearing' (the act of hearing Krishna's instruction). The engineering body simplifies toward Shankara's reading; this is a defensible choice (Shankara's is the more common reading and the one Prabhupada/Mukundananda follow), but a careful Sanskritist notes the simplification. The source pack flags this as the verse's most striking commentarial divergence, so the doctrinal honesty is preserved at the source-pack level even where the engineering body simplifies.

notes: The engineering layer correctly preserves: (a) the LAYERED closing condition (niścalā unmoving + acalā unwavering) — both Sanskrit terms appear in the engineering body and the gloss matches Shankara (niścalā = no longer pulled by external; acalā = no longer doubting once unmoving); (b) the PIVOT character of the verse — explicit reference to 'pivots the chapter' and 'sets up Arjuna's question at 2.54 (how does such a person speak, sit, walk?)'; (c) the closing condition (yogam avāpsyasi). The architecture-proposal scenario operationalizes the layered condition (the senior reads, evaluates, replies, but the team's sprint plan does NOT bend — niścalā; AND she does not internally re-litigate after the response — acalā). One minor framing note on the Shankara/Ramanuja śruti divergence; recorded but not critical. Pass.
